title: helianthemum polifolium subsp. pulverulentum
intro: Helianthemum polifolium subsp. pulverulentum, also known as the powdered rockrose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Cistaceae. It is a low-growing, woody shrub with small, pink or white flowers that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The powdered rockrose has been used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and fight off infections. It can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or salve, making it a versatile and accessible herbal remedy.
